ocr.text: In the study of families affected by hereditary breast and ovarian cancer, the data reveals various categories including BRCA1,
which comprises 24 families with 18 healthy carriers, 20 affected carriers, 13 non-carrier controls, totaling 51 individuals, with
a median age of 45 (range 23-78y), examined through SNP genotyping (51), expression studies (48), and dTL studies (30). The
BRCA2 cohort, consisting of 25 families, shows 27 healthy carriers, 21 affected carriers, 25 non-carrier controls, totaling 73
individuals, with a median age of 50 (range 22-87y), analyzed via SNP genotyping (73), expression studies (64), and dTL
studies (46).

The aBRCA1 + BRCA2 group includes 1 family with 1 healthy carrier, 2 affected carriers, 1 non-carrier control, totaling 4
individuals, median age 54 (range 42-61y), assessed through SNP genotyping (4), expression studies (4), and dTL studies (3). In
the b BRCAX category, involving 71 families, there are no healthy carriers, 74 affected carriers, 21 non-carrier controls,
totaling 95 individuals, with a median age of 49 (range 20-85y), evaluated through SNP genotyping (95), expression studies
(92), and dTL studies (53). The total FBOC data aggregates 121 families with 46 healthy carriers, 117 affected carriers, 60 non-
carrier controls, totaling 223 individuals, median age of 49 (range 18-87y), analyzed through SNP genotyping (223), expression
studies (209), and dTL studies (132). Furthermore, regarding RNA mapping, reliable single matches produced 4,061 tags at
54.3%, while reliable multiple matches yielded 2,865 tags at 38.3%, with 2,216 unreliable matches (29.6%) and 555 with no
match (7.4%), culminating in a total tag count of 7,481 (100.0%).
